channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DCEA Seizes 9.93 Tonnes of Illicit Drugs, Confiscates Narcotic Medicines and Destroys Cannabis Farms in Nationwide Operations
item.description: The Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) in Tanzania has carried out major operations across the country, seizing 9.93 tonnes of assorted illicit drugs. They also confiscated 1,795 bottles of...

item.title: How Iran’s Conflict Affects the Red Sea and Horn of Africa Region
item.description: The death of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on 28 February 2026, killed during U.S. and Israeli airstrikes in Tehran, has triggered a broader Middle East conflict. This war has direct an...

item.title: Highlights from the Australian Financial Review Business Summit 2026
item.description: The Australian Financial Review hosted its annual Business Summit this week, with sponsorship from BHP, attracting high-profile figures from business and politics. Key discussions included Fortescue c...

item.title: 11 Bulacan Vice Mayors Returning Home After Being Stranded in Dubai
item.description: Eleven vice mayors from Bulacan, who were stranded in Dubai during a personal trip in the Middle East, are now on their way back to the Philippines, according to Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla. The...

item.title: Brigade Commander Urges Troops in Southeast Operations to Maintain Professionalism and High Alert
item.description: The Commander of the 14 Brigade of the Nigerian Army Headquarters in Ohafia, Brigadier General Ahmad M. Dikko, don urge troops under him make dem continue to show strong professionalism and discipline...

item.title: Former CIA Director Warns Iran's Attacks on Civilians Could Escalate Regional Conflict
item.description: Former CIA Director General David Petraeus warned that Iran has made a strategic mistake by targeting civilians in the Middle East, in addition to attacking US and allied military bases. Speaking to S...

item.title: Governor Bala Mohammed welcomes Greek NGO Heart Doctors for humanitarian work in Bauchi
item.description: On Wednesday, Bauchi State Governor, Bala Mohammed, host top officials from the Greece-based NGO, Heart Doctors, for Iftar at his residence. Him wife, Mrs Aisha Bala Mohammed, alongside members of the...

item.title: UK Competition Watchdog Launches Investigation into Rising Private Dental Costs
item.description: The UK's £8 billion private dentistry sector is set to undergo an official investigation after reports of substantial price increases across services. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA), resp...

item.title: Canucks Put Up a Strong Effort Despite Loss to Hurricanes
item.description: The Vancouver Canucks displayed determination and resilience in their recent game against the Carolina Hurricanes, despite ultimately losing the match. Facing a team known for its offensive strength, ...

item.title: PCB issues warning over false reports on players’ conduct
item.description: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) on Thursday strongly condemned reports targeting the character of its players, calling them 'agenda-driven fake news' that will not be tolerated. This statement comes ...

item.title: Mail & Guardian Digital Edition Access Page - 06 March 2026
item.description: The webpage serves as a gateway for subscribers to access the Mail & Guardian's digital edition for Friday, 06 March 2026. It greets users with a friendly 'Good morning' message and invites them to cl...

item.title: Three People Arrested on Suspicion of Assisting Chinese Intelligence Released on Bail
item.description: UK police have released three people, including the husband of Labour Party MP Joani Reid, who were arrested on suspicion of spying for China. The arrests, conducted by counter-terrorism officers in L...

item.title: Tanzania Compensates Citizens for Wildlife Damage and Highlights Conservation Efforts
item.description: In Tanzania, the government has disbursed a total of 13.28 billion Tanzanian shillings to compensate 56,331 people affected by dangerous wildlife attacks between 2020 and 2025. Minister for Natural Re...

item.title: Three men, including husband of Labour MP Joani Reid, released on bail after arrests for suspected assistance to Chinese intelligence
item.description: Three men have been released on bail by the Metropolitan Police after being arrested on suspicion of assisting a foreign intelligence service, specifically linked to China, under Section 3 of the Nati...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran conflict as Iranian warship sunk and regional tensions rise
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate following the US sinking of an Iranian warship. Australian sailors were reportedly on board the submarine involved in the strike. In response, Iran has...

item.title: ADIAfrica Completes Free Eye Screening Program for Over 300 Residents in Rivers State
item.description: The Africa Global Development For Positive Change Initiative (ADIAfrica), together with Stephen Igwe and Judith Igwe International Educational System, don wrap up free eye screening outreach for peopl...

item.title: Taranjit Singh Sandhu appointed as Delhi's Lieutenant Governor following Vinai Kumar Saxena's transfer
item.description: Former Indian diplomat Taranjit Singh Sandhu has been appointed as the new Lieutenant Governor of Delhi, taking over from Vinai Kumar Saxena. The announcement was made on Thursday by Rashtrapati Bhava...

item.title: Court of Appeal upholds death sentence for man convicted of killing infant neighbour
item.description: In Arusha, the Court of Appeal has confirmed the death sentence for Mr Tluway Shauri who was convicted of murdering his neighbour’s three-month-old baby, John Reginald. The tragedy occurred after a qu...

item.title: Interfaith Tamar Campaign Significantly Reduces Gender-Based Violence in Eastern DRC
item.description: For many years, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) don suffer plenty violence against women, especially after the Second Congo War where rape become weapon and former child soldiers carry toxic id...

item.title: Minor Girl Brutally Assaulted in Bannu Over Wearing Boys' Clothing and Socialising with Boy
item.description: In a distressing case from Bannu district, a young minor girl faced severe violence in the Zairki area of Domail tehsil. A group of armed men reportedly abducted her before subjecting her to a brutal ...
